description: Fix history system - sessions save but content is blank (issue #6), and improve AI context persistence (issue #7)
steps: 1. DIAGNOSIS: The history system saves sessions via IPC events from renderer.js ('save-conversation-turn', 'save-session-context', 'save-screen-analysis'). These are emitted by gemini.js via sendToRenderer(). The issue is that gemini.js calls saveConversationTurn() which sends 'save-conversation-turn' to renderer, then renderer calls storage.saveSession(). However, gemini.js also directly uses 'sendToRenderer' and the data flow is: main->renderer->main (IPC round-trip). The data arrives correctly because storage.saveSession() in the main process is invoked via 'storage:save-session' IPC handler. The actual fix needed: in gemini.js saveConversationTurn() and saveScreenAnalysis(), ALSO call storage.saveSession() directly in the main process (not just relay through renderer). This ensures data persists even if renderer doesn't process the event.

2. In src/utils/gemini.js, modify saveConversationTurn() to ALSO call storage.saveSession() directly: storage.saveSession(currentSessionId, { conversationHistory: conversationHistory }). This way the data is saved both via IPC to renderer AND directly in main process.

3. In src/utils/gemini.js, modify saveScreenAnalysis() to ALSO call storage.saveSession() directly: storage.saveSession(currentSessionId, { screenAnalysisHistory: screenAnalysisHistory, profile: currentProfile, customPrompt: currentCustomPrompt }).
4. In src/utils/gemini.js, modify initializeNewSession() to ALSO call storage.saveSession() directly to persist the initial session metadata (profile, customPrompt) immediately.

5. For AI context persistence (issue #7): Add a new function in gemini.js called loadPreviousContext() that on session init, reads the last N sessions from storage.getAllSessions(), extracts their conversation summaries, and passes them as context to the AI system prompt. This gives the AI memory of past sessions.

6. In src/utils/gemini.js, modify the session initialization (initializeNewSession or wherever the system prompt is built) to append a context section from previous sessions: read the last 3 sessions using storage.getAllSessions() and storage.getSession(), extract key Q&A pairs, and append them to the system prompt as 'Previous session context'.
7. Create a new storage function getRecentSessionContext() in src/storage.js that returns summarized context from the last 3 sessions (last 5 turns from each). This provides cross-session memory.
8. In gemini.js setupGeminiIpcHandlers or the session setup flow, call getRecentSessionContext() and inject it into the system prompt when building it.
acceptance_criteria: When a session ends and user opens History, the session shows actual conversation content (not blank)
saveConversationTurn in gemini.js persists data directly to disk in addition to IPC relay
saveScreenAnalysis in gemini.js persists data directly to disk
AI sessions include context from the last 3 previous sessions in their system prompt
getRecentSessionContext() function exists in storage.js and returns useful prior context
verification: Run 'node -e "require('./src/storage.js')"' from /projects/sandbox/cd to validate storage.js parses correctly
Run 'node -e "require('./src/utils/gemini.js')"' from /projects/sandbox/cd to validate gemini.js parses correctly
Run 'npx prettier --check src/' to verify formatting compliance
findings: gemini.js cannot be require()'d in isolation due to missing @google/genai dependency (expected for Electron app), but node --check confirms syntax is valid. storage.js requires cleanly. All prettier checks pass.
